title: Motion Path, Masonry, Highlights, Composition & Field-Sizing
description: Five fresh CSS patterns: motion path orbits, native masonry grid, custom highlight layers, additive animation composition, and field-sizing content.

drops.title: CSS Motion Path Orbits
drops.description: Six colored orbs race along Bezier paths via CSS offset-path — no canvas, no JS animation loops.
drops.concept: CSS Motion Path (offset-path) lets elements travel along arbitrary SVG paths defined directly in CSS. Each orb has a unique path shape — circle, ellipse, figure-eight, heart, diagonal — and a different animation duration, creating a complex choreography with zero JavaScript. The technique uses offset-distance animating from 0% to 100% inside a standard @keyframes rule.
drops.source: MDN Web Docs — CSS offset-path property

interaction.mouse: Adjust speed, orb size, and glow via debug panel

drops.title: CSS Native Masonry Grid
drops.description: Cards pack naturally into variable-height columns using grid-template-rows: masonry — one CSS property, no JS.
drops.concept: CSS Grid's masonry value for grid-template-rows (Chrome 133+, Firefox with flag) enables true masonry layouts where items stack tightly in columns without leaving gaps. A JS column-fallback handles older browsers. The demo shows 14 cards of varying heights with a feature-detection badge showing whether native masonry or the JS fallback is active.
drops.source: MDN Web Docs — CSS Grid masonry layout

interaction.mouse: Adjust column min-width and gap via debug panel

drops.title: CSS Custom Highlights API
drops.description: Apply multi-layer text highlights via ::highlight(name) — keywords, dates, code, and quotes styled independently without touching the DOM.
drops.concept: The CSS Custom Highlights API (Chrome 105+, Firefox 119+) lets developers register named highlight objects against arbitrary text ranges using CSS.highlights.set(). The highlights are then styled in CSS via ::highlight(name) pseudo-elements supporting color, background-color, and text-decoration. This demo shows four simultaneous highlight layers plus a live search highlight — all updating in under 2ms with no DOM modification.
drops.source: Chrome Developers — CSS Custom Highlight API

interaction.keyboard: Type in search box to add a 5th highlight layer

drops.title: CSS Animation Composition: add
drops.description: Hover and press animations stack additively onto an idle breathe — no transform math, no override juggling.
drops.concept: CSS animation-composition: add (Chrome 112+) causes a new animation's effect to be added to the underlying animation rather than replacing it. A button has a base 'breathe' scale animation; on hover, a 'lift' translateY animation is added on top — the button both breathes AND lifts simultaneously. On press, a 'squish' scale is added on top of both. Without animation-composition: add, the hover animation would cancel the breathe entirely.

interaction.hover: Hover cards to see lift stack onto breathe
interaction.click: Press to squish on top of lift
interaction.toggle: Switch between add and replace modes to compare

drops.title: CSS field-sizing: content
drops.description: Inputs and textareas resize to fit their content with a single CSS property — no JavaScript, no scrollHeight tricks.
drops.concept: CSS field-sizing: content (Chrome 123+, Edge 123+, Safari 17.4+) makes form fields automatically size to their content. A single-line input grows horizontally as you type; a textarea grows vertically. Constraints like min-width, max-width, and max-height work naturally. The demo shows three patterns: an expanding inline input, a textarea with an SVG character-count ring, a tag/chip input with animated chips, and a code editor that auto-sizes.

interaction.keyboard: Type into any field to watch it resize — no JavaScript involved
interaction.click: Press Enter in tag input to add chips; click × to remove
